summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (630) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (657), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 528 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 626-629 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 652-655 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). 6667265 (mention-reachable, in the b85df4a..HEAD window) shifted every address after ~519 — the const (59) and its doc (55-58) sit above the edit and are unmoved; the later addresses re-read at HEAD, so every address carries.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1407) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1408-1412) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1410) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. 6667265 (mention-reachable, in the b85df4a..HEAD window) added the mention-reachable fixture and shifted this test down; addresses re-read at HEAD, so they carry.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at d1af9a5 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics; VERIFIER-TYPED's window touched graph.rs but not the const region (still 5 at graph.rs:59, cite still 2026-07-02 at 55-58). The park's subject is untouched, and may still be wrong. A build-side friction capture records the same disagreement; the human's to read, not plan's to drain. rescoped 4a33911.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at d1af9a5, holding on every clause: no v0.1 tag (only era tags), crate 0.1.0, release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 68fae5c..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.

summary: explain's dispatch body (515-634) moves into read.rs, whose codemap job is already 'the read verbs' resolution' — main.rs keeps only the CLI call

edit.description: Delete explain()'s dispatch body (515-634, current HEAD, reverify at pickup). The Command dispatch call site (503, `print!("{}", explain(&target)?)`) requalifies to whatever read.rs names the moved function — read.rs already exports a `pub fn explain` (218, the target-species resolution explain's own body dispatches to at its own line 619, `Ok(read::explain(...))`) — a real name collision the move must resolve (rename the moved corpus-assembly wrapper, e.g. read::explain_target, or fold its prelude into the existing read::explain if the signature can absorb it); name the choice in the commit body. By this point every helper explain() calls (partition_kind_rows, repo_file_set, embedded_features_by_kind, collect_directive_members) already lives in the library wherever MAIN-GATE-VERB-MODULE-EXTRACT landed it — read.rs calls that qualified path unchanged.
Gains explain's dispatch body verbatim (515-634) under its resolved name (see main.rs edit) — the module's own codemap job is already 'the read verbs' resolution' (architecture.md).

tests.asserts: the gauntlet corpus's gate/explain output is byte-identical across the move — location-only, no behavior change
existing explain/target-resolution coverage stays green calling the renamed or folded read.rs entry point — the split is location/naming-only, no resolution-behavior change

acceptance: explain's dispatch body lives in src/read.rs (its own 'read verbs' resolution' home) under a name that doesn't collide with read::explain's existing target-species resolution; main.rs's CLI dispatch calls it, no logic left behind. cargo test and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings green.
notes: Item 3 of inbox RULED (session, standing delegation, observed b4a6947): completes MAIN-JUDGE-VERB-HOME-RULING's second half, explain's piece — read.rs already IS the read-resolution home, no new module. Serialized behind MAIN-GATE-VERB-MODULE-EXTRACT (shared main.rs); the read::explain name collision is a real, disk-verified fact, not a hypothetical. scoped at 8104308.

summary: graph.rs's dead-plugin judge stops reading the bare ENABLEMENT_FIELD constant; kind.rs deletes it once both consumers are gone (decision 0042's retirement, completed)

per.section: The fix lands at the mechanism

edit.description: `dead_registration`'s `Registration::Enablement` arm (727-739, reading `crate::kind::ENABLEMENT_FIELD` at 728 and 737) is the constant's last consumer once json_manifest.rs's own two uses (JSON-MANIFEST-ENTRY-SHAPE-DISPATCH) are gone. `dead_registration` receives only `&Registration`/`&Features` — no `CollectionAddress`/kind data reaches this call site today (traced: `dead_channel_set` → `reachable`/`live_members`, both keyed by bare kind name off `main.rs:591-596`'s `registrations: BTreeMap<&str, Vec<Registration>>`, which drops `CustomKind::collection_address` when building the map). Two designs, a judgment call for build, named in the commit body: (a) widen `Registration::Enablement` to carry its own `field: String`, mirroring the sibling variants `DescriptionTrigger`/`PathsMatch`/`Event`'s existing per-variant field, populated from the enabled-plugins kind's declared `Scalar { field }` shape when `main.rs` builds the registration map; or (b) thread `CollectionAddress`/entry-shape data down through `reachable`/`live_members`/`dead_channel_set`/`dead_registration`'s signatures. Once resolved, delete `kind.rs`'s now-fully-unused `ENABLEMENT_FIELD` const (219, doc 211-218).

If design (a) is chosen: the registration-map construction (591-596) populates `Registration::Enablement`'s new `field` from the enabled-plugins kind's declared shape when building `registrations`. If design (b): the map (or a parallel one) carries `CollectionAddress` data through to `graph::reachable`'s call site — exact threading is the same build-time judgment call as the graph.rs edit.

tests.asserts: a_false_valued_entry_gates_its_member_off_every_channel (154) stays green — the disabled plugin is still found dead, the two enabled ones still live, unchanged behavior through whichever design lands

existing dead_registration inline tests (e.g. a_paths_match_glob_dies_only_when_no_repo_file_matches_it and its neighbors) stay green — the retirement is signature-only for the Enablement arm, no change to other channels

acceptance: graph.rs's dead-plugin judge reads the field name from kind data, never the bare ENABLEMENT_FIELD constant; kind.rs no longer declares it. cargo test and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings green.
notes: Derived from decision 0042, completing Consequences bullet 1's ENABLEMENT_FIELD retirement (graph.rs was not named in the decision's own text, which lists only kind.rs/json_manifest.rs — but ENABLEMENT_FIELD cannot retire while this file still reads it; the retirement's completion, not new scope). Last entry in the 0042 chain. scoped at efe64aa.

summary: kind.rs's self-contained layout-document reader (Layout family, 284-565, needs only extract) extracts to a new flat layout module — human-ruled split, the algebra stays whole

new.description: New flat module, model subsystem (architecture.md's codemap) — the layout-document reader moved verbatim from kind.rs: Layout (struct, 284-287), LayoutRegion (291-313), LayoutReading (322-337), LayoutMember (343-356), LayoutError (362-389), impl Layout (396-479, the read method), next_heading (484-491), read_collection_member (497-525), parse_edge_entries (532-538), slugify (544-559). Verified: depends only on crate::extract and std path/collection types, no CustomKind reference anywhere in the moved body. SATISFIES_EDGE_FIELD (kind.rs 394) stays behind — textually inside the moved range but logically CustomKind's own edge-slot vocabulary (used by CustomKind::edge_slots at kind.rs 894/904, and externally by drift.rs/main.rs), never read by Layout's own methods — layout.rs imports it from crate::kind rather than owning a copy.

edit.description: Delete the moved Layout family (284-390, 396-565 — SATISFIES_EDGE_FIELD at 394 is the one line in that span that stays). Content's Layout variant (141+) now wraps crate::layout::Layout.
Imports Layout/LayoutRegion from crate::kind (line 30) and uses them at 1095, 1701, 1715, 1758-1761 — requalify every reference to crate::layout, no re-export shim in kind.rs.
References kind::Layout (1269), kind::LayoutError (1262 doc), kind::Content::Layout (1434, 1782) — requalify the Layout/LayoutError references to crate::layout.

tests.asserts: existing layout-document read/gate coverage stays green — the move is location-only, no read-behavior change
edge-field parsing (satisfies among them) stays green — SATISFIES_EDGE_FIELD's cross-module wiring (layout.rs importing kind.rs's const) resolves identically

acceptance: src/layout.rs exists in the model subsystem carrying the Layout family verbatim; kind.rs no longer defines Layout/LayoutRegion/LayoutReading/LayoutMember/LayoutError/next_heading/read_collection_member/parse_edge_entries/slugify. cargo test and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings green.
notes: Human-ruled via inbox (600ffd5, kind-cohesion capture): layout reader → new flat layout module. MAIN-IMPORT-EDGES-DOC-PARAM-COLLAPSE shipped (cb4f989) — unblocked, but collides on main.rs with the now-open GRAPH-RESOLVED-EDGE-WALK-CONSOLIDATE; reserialized behind it. Also shares drift.rs with the now-open DRIFT-EMIT-OUTCOME-LABEL-ZERO-CONSUMER-PRUNE — single-tag blockedBy can't express both waits; the graph entry is queue-front, ships first regardless. scoped at 612f2a5.

summary: kind.rs's crate-wide glob-compilation cache (compile_glob/GLOB_CACHE/pins, 1223-1284) extracts to a new flat foundation module glob — human-ruled split, three external callers by its own doc

new.description: New flat module, foundation subsystem (architecture.md's codemap: leaf vocabulary, globset-only, no harness knowledge) — compile_glob (1236-1259, doc 1223-1234), the GLOB_CACHE/GLOB_COMPILES thread_locals (1261-1274), and glob_compile_count (1281-1283, doc 1276-1280), moved verbatim from kind.rs. compile_glob's own doc comment names its callers: import's discovery walk, coverage_note's governs leaf test, graph's paths-match liveness test, plus kind.rs's own internal use (owns_source, 952; Primitive::matches, 1503-1507).

edit.description: Delete the moved glob-cache block (1223-1284); requalify internal callers (owns_source's governs-glob match, 952; Primitive::matches, 1503-1507) to crate::glob::compile_glob.
Call site (447, crate::kind::compile_glob) requalifies to crate::glob::compile_glob.
Call site (946, crate::kind::compile_glob) and its doc comment naming the surface (933) requalify to crate::glob::compile_glob.
Call site (752) and its doc comment naming the surface (1932) requalify to crate::glob::compile_glob.
The import (26, use crate::kind::{CustomKind, compile_glob}) and three call sites (355, 363, 380) requalify to crate::glob::compile_glob.
Two call sites (147, 153, kind::glob_compile_count) requalify to glob::glob_compile_count — the count-pin itself is unaffected, location-only.

tests.asserts: glob_compile_count's existing hoist-by-count pin stays green under the new location — one compilation per distinct glob, unchanged semantics, only the module path moves

acceptance: src/glob.rs exists in the foundation subsystem carrying compile_glob/glob_compile_count verbatim; kind.rs no longer defines them, calling crate::glob instead. cargo test and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings green.
notes: Human-ruled via inbox (600ffd5, kind-cohesion capture, superseding this tick's own first-pass parked filing): glob cache → new flat foundation module (glob) — globset-only, knows no harness. Serialized behind KIND-LAYOUT-READER-MODULE-EXTRACT (shared kind.rs); also shares import.rs/coverage_note.rs with open IMPORT-ROLLUP-WRITER-PLACEMENT/COVERAGE-KNOWN-SURFACES-RELOCATE, both queue-earlier and ship first. scoped at 600ffd5.
